title: The Bradley Cooper Hall of Fame and the ‘House of Gucci’ Oscars Surge
format: Sean and Amanda remember Sidney Poitier, parse a chaotic week of SAG nominations, and build a 10-film Bradley Cooper Hall of Fame.

segments.heading: Remembering Sidney Poitier
segments.note: In memoriam, following his death last week

films.title: No Way Out
films.note: His first starring role, an ER doctor squaring off with Richard Widmark's racist patient. Set the template for the parts Hollywood kept giving him.

films.title: To Sir, with Love
films.note: One of three films he released this year, playing a teacher in a London school.

films.title: In the Heat of the Night
films.note: The second of the three. The legendary one.

films.title: Guess Who's Coming to Dinner
films.note: And the third — a whole trio in one year, at his commercial peak.

films.title: Lilies of the Field
films.note: The Best Actor Oscar win.

films.title: Paris Blues
films.note: Opposite Paul Newman and Joanne Woodward. Sean rates it highly.

films.title: The Slender Thread
films.note: Made early in Sydney Pollack's career too.

films.title: Buck and the Preacher
films.note: His directorial debut, a Western with Harry Belafonte and Ruby Dee.

films.title: Sneakers
films.note: The one people Sean and Amanda's age actually grew up with, though Sean thinks it undersells the rest of the career.

films.title: House of Gucci
films.note: Its ensemble got recognized, which Sean reads as evidence it's back in the Best Picture conversation. Amanda wonders if voters just liked the cast list.

films.title: The Power of the Dog
films.note: Three individual acting nods (Dunst, Cumberbatch, Smit-McPhee) but no ensemble nomination — precedent (Nomadland, Green Book) says that's not conclusive either way.

films.title: Being the Ricardos
films.note: Javier Bardem's Best Actor nod for playing Desi Arnaz surprised both hosts. No ensemble recognition for the rest of the cast.

films.title: Cyrano
films.note: Peter Dinklage was the presumed frontrunner Bardem's nomination seems to have bumped.

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: Bradley Cooper's Supporting Actor nod for an extended cameo is what prompted this episode.

films.title: Spencer
films.note: Kristen Stewart, the presumed Best Actress frontrunner, got snubbed entirely.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Blanked except for Ariana DeBose. Rachel Zegler's absence surprised both hosts.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: Ensemble nomination only, which the hosts read as its Best Picture spot being confirmed — not really a showcase for anyone's acting.

films.title: Coda
films.note: Ensemble nomination plus a Troy Kotsur nod for the Apple release.

films.title: Belfast
films.note: Katrina Balfe and the ensemble got in, but Ciárán Hinds, Judi Dench, Jamie Dornan and Jude Hill were all left out — leaves the hosts with no read on where it stands.

segments.heading: The Bradley Cooper Hall of Fame

films.title: Wedding Crashers
films.note: Comic villainy launched primarily at Vince Vaughn for two hours. In the conversation for great-douchebag-performance history.

films.title: The Hangover
films.note: Phil Wenneck. Still funny; some of it more uncomfortable now than in 2009.

films.title: Limitless
films.note: The proof-of-concept movie — the first time the show believed he could carry a high-concept thriller by himself, Robert De Niro in the back seat notwithstanding.

films.title: The Place Beyond the Pines
films.note: Chosen over Joker for the tenth spot. Sean's favorite of his films, and a little underrated.

films.title: Silver Linings Playbook
films.note: His first Best Actor nomination, running around Philadelphia in a trash bag.

films.title: American Hustle
films.note: Amanda doesn't love the movie, but he's doing a very committed coked-out performance and got nominated for it.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
films.note: Rocket Raccoon. Voice acting Amanda calls harder than it looks, even if she can't stand these movies otherwise.

films.title: American Sniper
films.note: Chris Kyle, and his first Best Picture producer credit — where the Warren Beatty ambitions start to show.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Actor, director, writer, producer, and by Sean's read possibly the pinnacle of the career so far.

films.title: Licorice Pizza
films.note: Roughly fifteen minutes of screen time that finally pays off years of scene-stealing cameos.

referenced.group: Considered for the Hall of Fame
films: He's Just Not That Into You
How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
